message:
perf(indexer): collapse final last_scan_ts + last_indexed_at writes into one ephemeral writer; drop dead historical-restart probe

Two cleanups in src/indexer/mod.rs that landed together because the
profile said both stages were paying cost they didn't need to.

1. Single ephemeral writer for the final watermark write
   The shutdown tail of `run_index` previously opened two ephemeral
   writers in sequence (each paying open + preflight + close), one
   to set `last_scan_ts` (when `performed_scan`) and one to set
   `last_indexed_at`. Both are tiny scalar writes against the same
   storage; there's no reason to take two writer slots. Collapse
   them into a single `with_ephemeral_writer(...)` call whose
   closure does:
       if performed_scan { writer.set_last_scan_ts(scan_start_ts)? }
       writer.set_last_indexed_at(now_ms)
   The two informational `tracing::info!` lines (one for the scan
   ts update, one for the "preserved scan ts" lexical-resume case)
   are hoisted out of the writer scope and emitted after the write
   succeeds, so log output is identical. Net effect at shutdown:
   one writer open / preflight / close instead of two.

2. Retire the historical-bundle restart probe
   `full_rebuild_requires_historical_restart` and its helper
   `count_meta_entries_like` are dead-coded out. The probe walked
   `discover_historical_database_bundles(db_path)`, counted
   `historical_bundle_progress:%` meta rows, and emitted a
   `tracing::warn!` if it found stale progress markers — and then
   *always* returned `Ok(false)`. Every caller already short-
   circuited on the false result, so the warn-only side effect
   was the only thing that ever happened. The same condition is
   now flagged at the salvage-decision site, which is where it
   matters operationally.
   Function signature kept (with `_`-prefixed unused params) so
   call sites don't need to change in this commit; helper
   `count_meta_entries_like` (only caller was this function) is
   removed entirely.

Net diff: 28 added, 64 removed.
